NIS2 Title: Risk analysis and information system security policies
ISO 27001:2022 Clauses: A.5.1 (Policies for information security); A.5.8 (Information security in project management); A.8.12 (Data leakage prevention)
NIST CSF v2.0 Controls: GV.OC-01 (The organizational mission is understood and informs cybersecurity risk management); ID.RA-01 (Vulnerabilities in assets are identified, validated, and recorded)
Implementation Guidance: Establish policies and conduct regular risk assessments to identify threats to information assets.

ISO 27001:2022 Clauses: A.5.24 (Information security incident management planning and preparation); A.5.25 (Assessment and decision on information security events); A.5.26 (Response to information security incidents)
NIST CSF v2.0 Controls: DE.AE-02 (Potentially adverse events are analyzed to better understand associated activities); RS.MA-01 (The incident response plan is executed in coordination with relevant third parties once an incident is declared)
Implementation Guidance: Set up a dedicated incident response capability, define reporting lines, and establish detection logs.

ISO 27001:2022 Clauses: A.5.29 (Information security during disruption); A.5.30 (ICT readiness for business continuity); A.8.13 (Information backup)
NIST CSF v2.0 Controls: RC.RP-01 (Recovery plans are executed during or after an incident); PR.IR-04 (Adequate resource capacity to ensure availability is maintained)
Implementation Guidance: Ensure regular backups, implement disaster recovery sites, and establish a crisis management committee.

ISO 27001:2022 Clauses: A.5.19 (Information security in supplier relationships); A.5.20 (Addressing information security within supplier agreements); A.5.21 (Managing information security in the ICT supply chain)
NIST CSF v2.0 Controls: GV.SC-01 (Supply chain risk management program is established); GV.SC-02 (Suppliers are identified, prioritized, and assessed)
Implementation Guidance: Assess supplier risks, enforce security requirements in contracts, and audit critical service providers.

NIS2 Title: Security in network and systems acquisition, development, and maintenance
ISO 27001:2022 Clauses: A.8.25 (Secure development life cycle); A.8.28 (Secure coding); A.8.29 (Security testing in development and acceptance)
NIST CSF v2.0 Controls: PR.PS-01 (Secure software development lifecycle is implemented); PR.PS-02 (Hardware/software integrity is verified)
Implementation Guidance: Implement SSDLC, perform vulnerability scanning and penetration testing, and establish vulnerability disclosure policies.

NIS2 Title: Policies and procedures to assess risk management effectiveness
ISO 27001:2022 Clauses: A.5.35 (Independent review of information security); A.5.36 (Compliance with policies, rules and standards for information security)
NIST CSF v2.0 Controls: GV.PO-01 (Policy for managing cybersecurity risks is established based on organizational context, cybersecurity strategy, and priorities and is communicated and enforced); ID.RA-03 (Risk assessments are updated regularly)
Implementation Guidance: Conduct internal security audits, run table-top exercises, and perform management reviews.

NIS2 Title: Basic cyber hygiene practices and cybersecurity training
ISO 27001:2022 Clauses: A.6.3 (Information security awareness, education and training); A.8.1 (User endpoint devices)
NIST CSF v2.0 Controls: PR.AT-01 (Security awareness training is provided to all users); PR.AT-02 (Specialized training is provided to high-risk roles)
Implementation Guidance: Run phishing simulations, conduct onboarding cybersecurity training, and enforce device hardening standards.

NIST CSF v2.0 Controls: PR.DS-01 (The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-at-rest are protected); PR.DS-02 (The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-in-transit are protected)
Implementation Guidance: Define key management policies, enforce TLS 1.3 for communications, and encrypt corporate storage.

NIS2 Title: Human resources security, access control, and asset management
ISO 27001:2022 Clauses: A.5.9 (Inventory of information and other associated assets); A.5.15 (Access control); A.6.1 (Screening); A.6.2 (Terms and conditions of employment)
NIST CSF v2.0 Controls: PR.AA-01 (Identities and credentials for authorized users, services, and hardware are managed by the organization); PR.AA-02 (Identities are proofed and bound to credentials based on the context of interactions)
Implementation Guidance: Enforce the principle of least privilege, conduct background checks on employees, and maintain an automated asset inventory.

NIS2 Title: Use of MFA, secured voice, video, and emergency communications
ISO 27001:2022 Clauses: A.5.17 (Authentication information); A.8.2 (Privileged access rights); A.5.16 (Identity management)
NIST CSF v2.0 Controls: PR.AA-03 (Multi-factor authentication is enforced); RS.CO-04 (Stakeholders are notified of response activities and their roles)
Implementation Guidance: Enforce phishing-resistant MFA, use encrypted enterprise collaboration systems (e.g. Signal, Teams with E2EE), and set up out-of-band communication channels.
